part.text: I’ll locate the target-version implementation of `ShapeRefiner::InferShapesForFunctionSubNode`, compare its local API shape to the provided 2.7 patch, then apply only the equivalent resource shape-copy fix.

state.output:
commit ee119d4a498979525046fba1c3dd3f13a039fbb1
Author: Daniel Ellis <danielellis@google.com>
Date:   Wed Jul 14 12:43:17 2021 -0700

    Fix segmentation fault in shape inference logic.
    
    When running shape functions, some functions (such as `MutableHashTableShape`)
    produce extra output information in the form of a `ShapeAndType` struct.  The
    shapes embedded in this struct are owned by an inference context that is
    cleaned up almost immediately; if the upstream code attempts to access this
    shape information, it can trigger a segfault.
    
    `ShapeRefiner` is mitigating this for normal output shapes by cloning them
    (and thus putting the newly created shape under ownership of an inference
    context that will not die), but we were not doing the same for shapes and
    types.  This commit fixes that by doing similar logic on output shapes and
    types.
    
    PiperOrigin-RevId: 384761124
    Change-Id: I07c0c42d29dfbb55bfa13ec1f09ef825fb0a1a1d

 tensorflow/core/common_runtime/shape_refiner.cc | 21 +++++++++++++++++++--
 1 file changed, 19 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)
This commit shows that there is a high probability that this code is new, so the corresponding code segment cannot be found in the old version.
You can call `viewcode` and `locate_symbol` to further check the results step by step. For newly introduced code, we consider that this hunk `need not ported`.

part.text: The equivalent target code is present in `tensorflow/core/common_runtime/shape_refiner.cc` with the same vulnerable direct transfer from `node_context` to `outer_context`. I’m applying the minimal fix there by rebuilding each `ShapeHandle` in the outer inference context before setting handle shapes/types.
